name: Llama 3.1 405B
note: First openly available model rivaling top closed frontier models on knowledge, math, and tool use; 128K context.
tpsNote: Far exceeds a single RTX 4090's 24GB VRAM even at Q4 (~230GB); needs multi-GPU or heavy CPU/disk offload, so no meaningful single-4090 tok/s figure is reported

results.notes: Instruct (corrected: original record mislabeled this as the base pretrained model; the 50.7 figure is the Instruct-model GPQA score in Meta's comparison table)
Current LMArena Elo (dynamic, decays as newer models are added; peaked much higher at launch)
0-shot acc, Instruction-tuned 405B row of the official HF model card's 'Instruction tuned models' benchmark table (directly re-fetched and confirmed verbatim: 'ARC-C | 0 | acc | ... | 96.9').
MBPP++ (EvalPlus-augmented) base version, 0-shot pass@1, Instruct 405B (confirmed verbatim: 'MBPP ++ base version | 0 | pass@1 | ... | 88.6').

Berkeley Function-Calling Leaderboard, 0-shot acc, Instruct 405B (confirmed verbatim: 'BFCL | 0 | acc | ... | 88.5'). Meta reports this as plain 'BFCL' (not a versioned v3 variant), so mapped to the 'bfcl' slug rather than 'bfcl-v3'.
Multilingual MGSM, chain-of-thought, 0-shot exact match, Instruct 405B (confirmed verbatim: 'Multilingual MGSM (CoT) | 0 | em | ... | 91.6').

LogicKor official leaderboard, model_id meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-405B-Instruct-FP8, default prompt strategy [score corrected during adversarial verify: proposed 8.8 -> 8.79, per verifier: Fetched https://lk.instruct.kr/ directly (two independent fetches, consistent results). The model meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-405B-Instruct-FP8 appears three times on the LogicKor leaderboard with different prompt strategies: - 1-shot: 8.84 - default: 8.79 - cot-1-shot: 8.45 The claim specifies "default prompt strategy" and model_id meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-405B-Instruct-FP8, both of which correctly match a real leaderboard row — but that row's score is 8.79, not 8.8. This is a small (0.01) discrepancy that looks like an unrounded/rounded transcription slip rather than a wrong-model or wrong-variant mixup (the 1-shot variant score of 8.84 is further off and not the source of the "8.8" figure; it's most likely 8.79 was rounded to 8.8 losing precision). Note: LogicKor is archived (as of Oct 2024) but the leaderboard data itself remains accessible and unchanged at the source URL, so the source is valid and reachable. Recommend correcting the stored score to 8.79 to match the source exactly.]

Instruct 405B, 0-shot pass@1, EvalPlus-augmented HumanEval (±5.8). From Table 18 ('Pass@1 scores on code generation benchmarks') of Meta's 'The Llama 3 Herd of Models' technical report (arXiv:2407.21783), which reports HumanEval, HumanEval+, MBPP, and MBPP EvalPlus(base) side by side. Not previously tracked — corpus only had the plain HumanEval (89.0) and MBPP EvalPlus/mbpp-plus (88.6) figures from the HF model card.

Instruct 405B, pass@1, original MBPP (Austin et al. 2021, Table 18 of arXiv:2407.21783) — distinct from the EvalPlus-augmented 'MBPP EvalPlus (base)' figure (88.6) already tracked under mbpp-plus. Shot count not explicitly stated by Meta for this column in the paper; likely 0-shot based on the paired 0-shot HumanEval/MBPP-EvalPlus figures in the same table.

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real. The cited HF page (meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-8B) does contain comparative benchmark tables covering 8B/70B/405B together, and all seven Instruct scores for 405B (MMLU 87.3, MMLU-Pro 73.3, GPQA 50.7, IFEval 88.6, HumanEval 89.0, GSM8K 96.8, MATH 73.8) are literally present and match. Corrected the GPQA row's notes: it is the Instruct-model score per the table, not 'Base pretrained model' as the original record stated (score itself, 50.7, is unchanged and correct). Live-fetched lmmarketcap.com/benchmarks/arena_elo and confirmed it currently shows exactly 1229 Elo for Llama 3.1 405B. Release date (2024-07-23), 128K context, and Llama 3.1 Community License confirmed via Meta's blog and GitHub model card.

[2026-07-16] Re-verified both flagged comparability findings against the primary source (arXiv:2407.21783, "The Llama 3 Herd of Models") rather than taking the existing entry notes at face value. (1) bfcl=88.5: Table 22 ("Zero-shot tool use benchmarks") reports Llama 3 405B at 88.5 ±1.5 on plain "BFCL (Yan et al., 2024)" alongside Nexus/API-Bank/API-Bench — the original/generic BFCL, not a versioned BFCL v3 score. BFCL v3 (multi-turn evaluation) launched Sept 2024, after Llama 3.1's July 23, 2024 release, and its current best-known score (~77.8, GLM-4.5) is below 88.5, which independently rules out a v3 attribution. The "bfcl" slug is correct as filed; no relabel needed. (2) mbpp=78.8: Table 18 ("Pass@1 scores on code generation benchmarks") lists four side-by-side columns for Llama 3 405B: HumanEval 89.0 ±4.8, HumanEval+ 82.3 ±5.8, MBPP 78.8 ±3.6, MBPP EvalPlus (base) 88.6 ±3.2. The flagged 78.8 is confirmed to be the plain original MBPP (Austin et al., 2021) column, fully distinct from the 88.6 MBPP EvalPlus figure already tracked separately under mbpp-plus. The "mbpp" slug is correct as filed; no relabel needed. Both entries' current notes already reflected these clarifications (added in the prior 2026-07-15 audit pass); this pass independently re-confirmed them against the primary source PDF (Table 18 and Table 22) rather than relying on the notes text alone. No changes made to the results array.
